---
title: Subscribe!!
date: 2026-03-26
draft: false
---


# Your Task

> Never miss the latest post on The Median Duck.
>
> Most up-to-date reader wins.
>
> Your Time Starts Now!


```{r, echo = FALSE, include = FALSE}
library(here)
here("static")

preamble_dir <- here("static", "code", "R", "preamble")
preamble_file  <- "post_preamble.R"

source(file.path(preamble_dir, preamble_file))
```


# A Little (Alex Horne) Hack
```{r, echo = FALSE, cache = FALSE}
follow_it_file <- file.path(here(), "layouts", "partials", "follow_it.html")
htmltools::includeHTML(follow_it_file)
```


:::{.infobox .today}
Despite entering this information on The Median Duck site itself, we do not have access to the e-mail address supplied.
:::


# A Josh Widdicombe Counting Baked Beans Approach 

:::{.infobox .today}

1. Choose your preferred RSS feed e-mailer platform: [feedrabbit.com](https://feedrabbit.com/) or  [blogtrottr](https://blogtrottr.com/).
    - Signing up to these services may be required.
2. In the *"URL of a feed or web site"* input field (or similarly labelled), provide the following address: [https://bluevolvo87.github.io/themedianduck/index.xml](https://bluevolvo87.github.io/themedianduck/index.xml).

:::

**Some considerations: **

- [feedrabbit.com](https://feedrabbit.com/) is my preferred platform in my opinion.
    - The e-mails are minimalistic, with no-ads embedded into e-mail.
    - However, the feed does appear to be monitored less and you may not get the latest post e-mail as quickly (a few hours delay).
- [blogtrottr](https://blogtrottr.com/) does seem to be quicker in providing update e-mails.
    - However, the e-mails are less streamlined with ads embedded into the e-mail.

# Terms and Conditions

- The Median Duck takes no responsibility in readers usage of these third party RSS e-mailers.
- The Median Duck  does not collect or have access to the e-mail addresses that are supplied.
    - The Median Duck has no intention of collecting such information at a later date as it respects the privacy of our readers.
- Readers are free to unsubscribe from these e-mailers through their chosen RSS e-mailer. Typically there's an *"unsubscribe"* button at the bottom of their e-mailers.


```{r, out.width = "50%", fig.cap = "The Bookish Duck (Image Courtesy of [ducksinthewindow.com](https://ducksinthewindow.com/library-reader-rubber-duck/))"}
knitr::include_graphics("https://cdn11.bigcommerce.com/s-nf2x4/images/stencil/1280x1280/products/842/7847/Library-Reader-Rubber-Duck-Yarto-2__48932.1670167131.jpg?c=2")
```
